channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Sanctions Network Exploiting Migrants and Supplying Russian Drone Industry
item.description: Britain sanctioned 35 people and entities involved in recruiting vulnerable migrants to fight for Russia against Ukraine and produce drones for the conflict. The measures aim to disrupt Russia's drone production capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Steps Up Medium-Range Drone Strikes on Russian Forces
item.description: Ukrainian troops are rapidly increasing medium-range drone strikes on Russian forces, President Zelenskiy announced, demonstrating growing capability as Kyiv fights Moscow in the fifth year of the war.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia Limits Mobile Internet Before Scaled-Back Victory Day Parade
item.description: Russia cut off mobile internet services in Moscow ahead of the annual May 9 parade commemorating victory over Nazi Germany, which has been scaled back due to threats of drone attacks from Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇨🇳 Trump and Xi to Discuss Taiwan in Upcoming Talks
item.description: Taiwan is likely to be a topic of conversation between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping in upcoming discussions, according to U.S. officials.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkey Ramps Up Steel Dome Air Defense System Production
item.description: Turkish defense firm Aselsan will ramp up delivery of parts for Turkey's integrated multi-layer Steel Dome air defense system in 2026, strengthening the country's indigenous air defense capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Denies UAE Attacks, Warns of Crushing Response
item.description: Iranian military command denied attacking the UAE, while warning it reserves the right to respond. The denial comes amid ongoing tensions in the Gulf following US attempts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Times of Israel

item.title: 🇩🇪🇮🇱 German FM Backs Israel Presence in South Lebanon
item.description: German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul said Israel has every right to be in south Lebanon, calling the ongoing operation necessary, but warned of the damages of war.
item.dc:creator: Times of Israel

item.title: 🤖🛡️ NATO Needs Policies for Sharing AI-Generated Intel
item.description: A NATO official said new policies are needed to share AI-generated intelligence across all 32 alliance members, as current arrangements rely on workarounds and exceptions.
item.dc:creator: Defense News

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia Declares Victory Day Ceasefire, Ukraine to Observe Truce Earlier
item.description: Russia announced a unilateral ceasefire for Victory Day celebrations on May 9-10, but threatened massive strikes on Kyiv if Ukraine disrupts the parade. Zelenskyy responded that Ukraine would observe a truce starting Wednesday, while noting the scaled-down Moscow parade without tanks shows Russian weakness.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 🇨🇦 Alberta Separatists Submit Signatures for Independence Referendum
item.description: Alberta separatists delivered nearly 302,000 signatures to force a provincial referendum on leaving Canada, exceeding the 178,000 threshold. Premier Danielle Smith says she will proceed if signatures are verified, though she personally opposes separation. Indigenous groups are challenging the move in court.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: ⚖️ Supreme Court Restores Telehealth Access to Abortion Pill Mifepristone
item.description: The Supreme Court temporarily blocked a lower court ruling that had restricted mifepristone access, restoring telehealth and mail-order prescriptions for the abortion pill. The order remains in effect for a week while the court considers the case, which Louisiana brought challenging FDA safety rules.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 🇸🇦🇦🇪 Gulf States Have Mixed Feelings About US After Iran War, Expert Warns
item.description: F. Gregory Gause III, Professor Emeritus at Texas A&Ms Bush School, says Gulf states are conflicted about the US role in the region as the Iran war reshapes alliances and power dynamics.
item.dc:creator: CNN

item.title: 🇺🇳🇮🇷 US-Bahrain Draft UN Resolution Seeks Sanctions Over Iran Strait of Hormuz Attacks
item.description: The US and Bahrain are pushing a UN Security Council draft resolution that could authorize sanctions or force against Iran if it fails to halt attacks on commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇳🇱⛽ Dutch Gas Prices Edge Lower as US Moves to Open Strait of Hormuz
item.description: European natural gas prices fell as US efforts to weaken Iranian control over the Strait of Hormuz raised hopes for improved energy supply flows through the critical shipping lane.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇷🇺💰 Russian Court Seizes Billionaires Stake in Farming Giant for State
item.description: A Russian court ruled billionaire Vadim Moshkovich must transfer his stake in Rusagro to the state in another high-profile asset grab targeting oligarchs.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇷🇺⚖️ Two Russian Hypersonic Scientists Get 12 Years for Treason
item.description: Two Russian physicists involved in hypersonic missile research were convicted of treason and sentenced to 12.5 years each in a penal colony in closed-door trials.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel signals return to war in Gaza as ceasefire talks stall
item.description: Israel has expanded military control over 60% of Gaza while hardening disarmament demands, with analysts warning the Mladenov peace proposal is deliberately geared toward rejection to justify renewed assault. Senior Israeli military officials say the best time to defeat Hamas is now.
item.dc:creator: New Arab

item.title: 🇹🇭🇰🇭 Thailand cancels 25-year oil pact with Cambodia after border clashes
item.description: Thailand scrapped a 2001 agreement for joint offshore energy exploration with Cambodia, forcing Phnom Penh to pursue UN arbitration. The move follows armed conflict between the neighbors last year.
item.dc:creator: SCMP

item.title: 🇹🇩⚔️ Boko Haram kills 23 Chadian soldiers in Lake Chad attack
item.description: Boko Haram militants attacked a military base on Lake Chad, killing at least 23 troops and wounding dozens more. President Deby vowed to eradicate the group despite claiming it had no sanctuary in Chad last year.
item.dc:creator: DW

item.title: 🇺🇦⚖️ Ukraine corruption probe shadows top US negotiator
item.description: Ukraine National Security Council chief Rustem Umerov faces renewed scrutiny over leaked tapes linking him to a major corruption scandal, even as he leads delicate peace negotiations with Washington and Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Kyiv Independent

item.title: 🇧🇷🇦🇪✈️ Brazil Embraer Eyes New Middle East Defense Deals After UAE Order
item.description: Brazilian aerospace company Embraer expects new defense aircraft deals in the Middle East, including government-to-government procurements, following a landmark C-390 military plane order from the UAE.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇦🇪🇮🇷⚔️ UAE Under Iranian Missile and Drone Attack as US Says Ceasefire Holds
item.description: The United Arab Emirates reported Iranian missile and drone attacks even as Washington maintained a shaky US-Iran ceasefire was intact. US forces exchanged fire while attempting to keep the Strait of Hormuz open for shipping.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺💀 Russian Attacks Kill at Least 20 Before Rival Ceasefires Take Effect
item.description: Russia and Ukraine proposed competing ceasefire dates around May 9 victory celebrations. Russian drone and missile strikes killed at least 20 people before any truce could begin, with Zelensky condemning Moscow's "cynicism."
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: 🇺🇸💰📉 US Trade Deficit Widens as AI Investment Boom Drives Imports Higher
item.description: The US trade deficit widened in March as an artificial intelligence investment surge pulled in imports, offsetting increased exports including crude oil shipments amid the Middle East conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇹🇷🛡️ Turkey Accelerates Delivery of Steel Dome Air Defense System Components
item.description: Turkish defense firm Aselsan will ramp up deliveries of parts for Turkey's integrated multi-layered "Steel Dome" air defense system in 2026, strengthening national air defense capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Reuters
